thinking_styles.description: Monolithic single-mind framing — the user reasons end-to-end as one unit, no internal decomposition surfaced.
Generate-then-merge framing — the user produces multiple independent candidates and combines them at the end.
Decompose-and-delegate framing — the user splits the task into sub-parts and asks for each to be handled, then integrated.
Compare-and-converge framing — the user weighs multiple perspectives or trade-offs and asks for a consensus answer.
Decompose-with-coordination framing — the user splits sub-tasks but explicitly asks the parts to talk to each other, not only via a central planner.

queries.independent: Could you draft a few full end-to-end trip plans for me, each one produced separately and on its own without any internal sub-task split, and then merge the strongest pieces of those drafts into one final itinerary? It is a 3-day trip for one person from Washington to Myrtle Beach, running from March 13th to March 15th, 2022, with a total budget of $1,400.
queries.centralized: Could you break this trip into its sub-parts, transit, lodging, and meals plus attractions, work each one out on its own, and then combine the pieces into one unified final plan? It is a 3-day trip for one person from Washington to Myrtle Beach, running from March 13th to March 15th, 2022, with a total budget of $1,400.
queries.decentralized: Could you look at this plan from several angles, the cheapest option, the most convenient option, and the most rule-safe option, weigh the trade-offs against each other, and converge on the version that holds up best across those criteria? It is a 3-day trip for one person from Washington to Myrtle Beach, running from March 13th to March 15th, 2022, with a total budget of $1,400.
queries.hybrid: Could you split the work into sub-tasks but have flights, lodging, and meals coordinate directly with each other, so flight times match check-in windows and the category budgets balance, rather than routing every decision through a single coordinator? It is a 3-day trip for one person from Washington to Myrtle Beach, running from March 13th to March 15th, 2022, with a total budget of $1,400.
